definition_en: A person who rents out a property to tenants
example: The landlord increased the rent by 10%.

definition_en: A person who pays rent to live in a property owned by someone else
example: The tenant signed a one-year lease.

definition_en: A loan used to buy a property, repaid over many years
example: They took out a mortgage to buy their first home.

definition_en: The process of repairing and improving a building
example: The kitchen renovation took three months.

definition_en: A machine used in the home, such as a washing machine or fridge
example: All kitchen appliances are included in the rent.

definition_en: Bills for electricity, gas and water used in a home
example: Utility bills are not included in the monthly rent.

definition_en: The space just below the roof of a house, often used for storage
example: We found old boxes in the attic.

definition_en: A room or level below ground level in a building
example: They converted the basement into a home office.

definition_en: Material used to keep heat inside a building
example: Good insulation reduces heating bills significantly.

definition_en: Containing furniture provided by the landlord
example: The flat is fully furnished with everything you need.

definition_en: A legal agreement to rent a property for a specific period
example: She signed a two-year lease on the apartment.

definition_en: A person whose job is to sell or rent properties for others
example: The estate agent showed us three flats.

definition_en: A house that is not joined to any other house
example: They moved to a detached house with a large garden.

definition_en: One of a row of houses joined together on both sides
example: Victorian terraced houses are common in London.

definition_en: Money paid at the start of a tenancy as security
example: He paid a deposit of one month's rent.

definition_en: The work of keeping a building in good condition
example: The landlord is responsible for general maintenance.

definition_en: A part added to a building to make it larger
example: They built an extension to add another bedroom.

translation: baño en suite / habitación con baño privado
definition_en: A bedroom with its own private bathroom
example: The master bedroom has an en suite bathroom.

definition_en: A lot of objects in a messy, untidy state
example: She decided to clear the clutter from the spare room.

definition_en: A space under the roof of a building, often converted into a living area
example: They converted the loft into a bedroom.

definition_en: A covered entrance area at the front or back of a house
example: She waited on the porch while he opened the door.

definition_en: The furnishing and decoration style of a room or building
example: The hotel's modern decor impressed all the guests.
